steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls between the subject ‘She’ and the direct object ‘them from willow and sedge.’ That position requires the sentence’s main verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — weaving: a gerund or present participle — not a finite verb, cannot anchor the sentence. ✗
  • Choice B — weaves: third-person singular simple present, agrees with ‘She’ and works as the main verb. ✓
  • Choice C — to weave: an infinitive — non-finite, cannot serve as main verb. ✗
  • Choice D — to be weaving: a perfect participle — non-finite, cannot serve as main verb. ✗
B is correct.
